title: Policy evasion through staged research decomposition

evidence.observed_behavior: A user decomposes a disallowed request into apparently harmless subtasks distributed across multiple agent sessions.
evidence.why_it_matters: A single-turn policy check may miss harmful intent that appears only across the task graph.
open_questions: Can intent aggregation preserve privacy while detecting staged abuse?
Which task joins should be reviewed by a human?

competing_hypotheses: The decomposition is normal research planning.
The decomposition is intentional policy evasion.
recommended_action: Create eval cases for staged requests and add task-graph review for suspicious decomposition patterns.

human_approval.reason: No external side effect or sensitive access observed in this trace; monitor for escalation.

residual_risk: Low-confidence weak signal may be under-prioritized until pattern repeats.
reusable_eval_case: Multi-session decomposition of an unsafe request into harmless-looking subtasks.
taxonomy_gap: Need evidence standard for intent aggregation across sessions.
